notes.note: Rock-solid under the high ball, and a try on the stroke of halftime give a great indication into Jesse Arthars role and job security over the next month. The Broncos stand-in fullback is now a SC buy with plenty of upside over the coming weeks.
Jesse Arthars replaces Reece Walsh at fullback and could hold the position over the next month. One Bronco to watch while Walsh is sidelined with injury.
Passed the eye test in his NRL debut. 'AJ' Jones could find a home in a Bulldogs' forward pack lacking some substance. One to watch if he can receive more game time.

A late try assist helped Jake Averillo match a positive match up the Sea Eagles. With all the attention on Herbie Farnworth, Averillo and his increased attacking opportunities have gone widely unnoticed. POD play at a premium price.

Scoring tries for fun - Jake Averillo extends his scoring streak with another try overnight. Perhaps the forgotten man in the Dolphins backline - he has seen an uptick in base scoring and opportunities with a more balanced attack. Mega POD?

A simplified role on the left edge at five-eighth proved fruitful at times for Jayden Campbell. The lively playmaker got better as the game went on. While an adjustment, it could add value to Campbell especially if he can earn DPP status during the season.

A second half try assist to Jojo Fifita was as good as it got for Jayden Campbell on Saturday afternoon. It was a sorry day for the entire Titans' attack who missed the jump early. Campbell is one of the few top halfbacks to offer round 12, and 15 coverage. Worth considering in a couple of weeks.

A suffocating Warriors' defence limited Jayden Campbell, with the exception of a line break from a Cooper Bai offload. Owners now have a glimpse at Campbell's scoring floor. Over the last three seasons, Campbell has scored less than 40 on three occasions, one was due to injury. Look for a rebound following the bye.

POD pick up Jayden Campbell crushed the Eels on his way to two tries. New owners who selected the Titans' halfback are immediately rewarded given how Nathan Cleary and Nicho Hynes fared this week. No matter how poor the Titans' are, Campbell seems to score well. Injury history is the only concern. Non-owners could hold up until after the Titans' round 8 bye.

A late try assist got Jayden Campbell on the board. It's been an outstanding start for the gun playmaker, considering the Titans' attack. As their schedule tightens up, its worth keeping tabs on Campbell and his output. Make a call after the Titans' week 8 bye.

Jayden Campbell returned from a hamstring injury and made an immediate impact, scoring two first half tries. All good things that happen with the Titans' attack centre around Campbell. He will be a hot target following the Titans' round eight bye.
Remained on the right wing despite Sua Fa'alogo leaving the field with a HIA. Peel has seen little attacking opportunities so far. He has limited appeal as a cheapie at this time.

A tough NRL debut for Hugo Peel who saw little opportunities in attacking positions. Peel does offer speed out wide which is likely to keep in him the team short-term. The Storm's attacking woes will hinder any appeal for Hugo over the next fortnight.
First career try and a 42-point base stat effort in the centres. Should Moses Leo miss extra time, Manaia Waitere could be a downgrade option.

Quality touches from Bradman Best who set up three tries to Greg Marzhew on Saturday afternoon. The powerful centre crossed second try of the season, posting his third highest SC to date. The Knights' attack has found its feature with the return of Ponga, Lucas and Best. Round 12 and 15 byes will limit appeal, however best has POD appeal with upside for H2H players.

Three try assists for Bradman Best in a powerful return from injury. With the Knights getting the band back together on the left edge, Best and Marzhew capitalised. It was an Origin audition. Hopes of Best leaking plenty of cash have been quickly dashed.

A first half blitz catered for the majority of Bradman Best's scoring in round two.  Two try assists and a line break showcased Best's attacking ability. After Ponga and Brown went down with injury, so did the Knights' opportunities with the ball. Owners may need to temper expectations over the next month without Ponga feeding the left edge.

A category one HIA has ended Blayke Brailey's night. The Sharks captain will now be unavailable for magic round. It was a promising start as Brailey looked the most dangerous of the Sharks' spine before exiting.

Raise the bat. Consecutive centuries overnight in a point-a-thon in Townsville. Blayke Brailey has shown the ability to kick-start the Sharks attack through his running game - points often come of the back of it. A subtle reminder the Origin selectors that he is ready. Set and forget at this point in time.

A three try assist first half sees Blayke Brailey post his first 100-point effort since round 20 last season. Seven try assists of the season. His decision making around the ruck has been key. Brailey is a must-have HOK following the Sharks' round seven bye.

Fourth try assist of the season props up Brailey's scoring following a quiet running game. It was Brailey's lowest base output of the season as both teams found success on the edges. The Sharks' HOK is a set and forget option.

60-points in base and a try assist as Brailey's work-rate a standout. Last season the Sharks' hooker averaged 45-points in base, a huge uptick in 2026. Hopes of Brailey leaking cash to start the season have diminished. He remains a strong buy option heading into round four.

HOK focus: Blayke Brailey has averaged 55-points in base stats through the opening two rounds. This is a 10-point increase on last season. While it is only a small sample size, there is a pattern that two gun hookers will be most valuable this season. Non-owners could move early on a Brailey/Grant combo.
A strong middle performance in a match with limited attacking opportunities for the Sharks. With Harry Grant firing at the start of the season, Brailey is the #2 HOK available.

There is no slowing Blayke Brailey in 2026. 55+ in base, including an effective running game thatechoed his breakout 2025 campaign. There is optimism around the return of the HOK position under the new scoring system. A Brailey/Harry Grant combo is quickly becoming a reality.
